extraction.one_line_summary: HEALY Ben soloed away with 50km remaining on Salita Capuccini, winning by 1:49 while ROGLIČ attacked EVENEPOEL on final climb taking 14 seconds.
tactical_patterns: HEALY attacked from breakaway at 50km to go on Capuccini climb, exploiting early positioning before finale hardened.
ROGLIČ attacked EVENEPOEL on final climb while THOMAS/GEOGHEGAN HART worked to stay on wheel, revealing INEOS dual-leader strategy.
DSM controlled peloton for MILAN's Ciclamino defense despite sprint finish being impossible, wasting domestique energy unnecessarily.
EVENEPOEL showed weakness on final climb day before crucial TT, suggesting vulnerability when defending rather than attacking.

rider_signals.HEALY Ben: positive: Won by 1:49, biggest Giro margin in 5 years, attacking from breakaway 50km out shows exceptional diesel engine.
rider_signals.ROGLIČ Primož: positive: Took 14 seconds on EVENEPOEL on final climb, showing superior climbing form before individual TT.
rider_signals.EVENEPOEL Remco: negative: Lost 14 seconds to ROGLIČ on final climb day before his specialty TT, first visible weakness of race.
rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Followed ROGLIČ attack successfully alongside GEOGHEGAN HART, INEOS deploying two-card GC strategy effectively.
rider_signals.GEOGHEGAN HART Tao: positive: Matched ROGLIČ alongside THOMAS on final climb, confirming dual GC protection for INEOS.
rider_signals.GEE Derek: positive: Second place 1:49 behind HEALY from breakaway, showing consistent stage-hunting form.
rider_signals.ZANA Filippo: positive: Third place finish from breakaway, strong positioning throughout fragmented break.
rider_signals.BARGUIL Warren: positive: Fourth place from breakaway, demonstrating still-relevant climbing ability in reduced group.
rider_signals.MILAN Jonathan: neutral: Extended Ciclamino lead minimally despite DSM controlling for him, marginal gains for significant team effort.
